v8_review:
SUMMARY:
This PR refactors the preferences system by replacing the `preferences` package with a new `settings` package using an interface-based design (`SettingsEditor`/`SettingsAsPreferencesEditor`), removing duplicate GUI-specific preference classes, and consolidating job/history retrieval into `JobService`. While the architectural goals are sound, the migration introduces two breaking changes that will silently degrade existing user experience: loss of the `ALWAYS_AUTO_PASTE` preference and corruption of persisted `Folders` JSON data.

POINTS:
- [Core/src/main/java/settings/SettingsEditor.java, Core/src/main/java/settings/AppSettings.java] **`ALWAYS_AUTO_PASTE` preference silently dropped.** The old `gui.preferences.Labels` enum included `ALWAYS_AUTO_PASTE`, and `UIController.isAutoPaste()` returned `form.cbAutoPaste.isSelected() || AppSettings.GET.alwaysAutoPaste()`, providing a global override that forced auto-paste ON regardless of the checkbox. The new settings system has no equivalent method. After migration, users who relied on `ALWAYS_AUTO_PASTE` will lose that behavior; auto-paste will depend solely on the per-session checkbox. Either add `isAlwaysAutoPaste()`/`setAlwaysAutoPaste()` to `SettingsEditor` and `AppSettings`, or explicitly document this as intentional feature removal.

- [GUI/src/main/java/gui/support/Folders.java:66–80] **Folders JSON serialization format change breaks existing user data.** The old code serialized the `Folders` object: `gson.toJson(folders)` → `{"folders":["/path/1","/path/2"]}`. The new code serializes the `LinkedList<String>` directly: `GSON.toJson(folders)` → `["/path/1","/path/2"]`. The new `readFolders()` method calls `GSON.fromJson(json, String[].class)`, which throws `JsonSyntaxException` when encountering the old `{"folders":[...]}` format. The exception is caught but only logged — the user's saved folder list silently becomes empty. Add backward compatibility: attempt to parse as `String[]` first, and on `JsonSyntaxException`, try parsing as a wrapper object with a `folders` field (or use `JsonElement` to inspect whether the root is an array or object).

- [Core/src/main/java/support/DownloadConfiguration.java:270–271, GUI/src/main/java/ui/UIController.java:130–134,146–150,507–511,519–523,576–580,635–641] **Commented-out dead code from Jobs manipulation.** The PR correctly identifies that `JobService.getJobs()` returns a fresh `Jobs` object each call, making `.setList()`, `.remove()`, `.add()`, `.clear()` calls ineffective. However, the old code was identically broken — `preferences.Get.jobs()` also created a new object per call. The commented-out blocks with `/* no effect ... */` annotations are dead code clutter. Consider either: (a) removing the commented blocks entirely and updating the design so `JobService` returns a managed singleton that persists changes, or (b) documenting the known limitation more clearly. Leaving commented-out code with explanations is not better than leaving the broken working code.

- [Core/src/main/java/settings/SettingsAsPreferencesEditor.java:164–170] **`flush()` called only on `setDriftyUpdateAvailable()`.** The old code implicitly relied on `Preferences` auto-flushing or periodic saves. The new code calls `PREFERENCES.flush()` explicitly only inside `setDriftyUpdateAvailable()` (line 167). All other setters skip flushing, relying on JVM termination to persist. If the application crashes before JVM shutdown, settings like theme, folders, auto-paste, etc. will be lost. Consider flushing in a general `save()` method or using `Preferences.addPreferenceChangeListener()` to auto-flush on changes.

- [GUI/src/main/java/ui/UIController.java:609] **`isAutoPaste()` no longer checks persistent preference.** Now returns only `form.cbAutoPaste.isSelected()`. Combined with the `ALWAYS_AUTO_PASTE` removal, the checkbox state is purely ephemeral — toggling it in one session does not persist to the next. The `Settings.java` listener (`createAutoPasteCheck()`) calls `AppSettings.setGuiAutoPasteEnabled(newValue)` on toggle, but `isAutoPaste()` ignores this value. The checkbox's persisted state in settings is disconnected from the runtime check.

- [Core/src/main/java/module-info.java] **`exports data` added for new `JobService` package.** The `data` package is newly exported, but only `JobService` is defined there. Verify that `data.JobService` is the intended public API for both CLI and GUI modules, or consider restricting exports if this is an internal implementation detail.
